source: A fault has occurred. Press the St button on the left display and look up the fault code in the manual.
AFB is inactive. Release the Train Brake and Electric Brake to recover AFB control.
Battery power must be enabled before attempting to drive this locomotive.
Close the LZB/PZB Cut-Off Valve to release the emergency brake when the Circuit Breaker is Open.
Close the Main Circuit Breaker to draw power from the overhead wires.
Close the Sifa Cut-Off Valve to release the emergency brake when the Circuit Breaker is Open.
Cycle the Throttle between OFF and MIN twice to release the traction lock.
Ensure that all disconnected Brake Pipe Air Hoses have their Angle Cocks closed or the Brake Pipe will not charge!
Ensure that all disconnected Main Reservoir Pipe Air Hoses have their Angle Cocks closed or the Main Reservoir will not charge!
Formation Speed Limit Is Lower Than Timetable Speed Limit
Hold the Electric Train Supply in START for one second to energize ETS and keep coach batteries charged.
In case of Battery Main Reservoir Valve faults, open the Main Reservoir Override Valve in the machine room.
MU Train Control Fault. Only one cab can be active and all locomotives must have compatible modes.
MU Train Control is indicating that the brakes (Air Brake or Parking Brake) have not released on the rear vehicle.
Manually switch master HSM in the MTD display if the brake computer is malfunctioning.
NBÜ/ep Brakes Available on All Wagons
Number of Direct Release Control Valves
Number of Graduated Release Control Valves
Number of K, L or LL Composite Brakes
Press and hold to change the display language to English
Press and hold to change the display language to English
Press to auto complete brake slip.
Quick Release must only be used for short durations or it may lead to stuck brakes.
Release the Direct Brake while moving to prevent Penalty Brake applications.
Release the Parking Brake before attempting to depart.
Secondary Letters h, z or ee
Set the Throttle to OFF to release the traction lock.
Successfully performed a full brake test
The Pantograph Switch must not be in DOWN & SAND in any cab if attempting to raise the pantograph.
The Pantograph must be raised before attempting to drive this locomotive.
The pressure in the Pantograph/MCB reservoir must be at least 4.8 bar before the Pantograph can raise.
The pressure in the Pantograph/MCB reservoir must be at least 7.0 bar before the MCB can be closed.
Train Data panel is in Basic Data mode. Speed will be limited until a valid train data is input.
Turn on Windshield Heating to defog the windshield.
Wait a moment between Electric Braking and applying Power to prevent ASG malfunctions.
Wait until the Power Converters have finished starting up before drawing power.
